idea.hook: Travel to the most LUMINOUS objects in the universe?
facts: Quasar 3C 273 is one of the brightest known objects, fueled by a supermassive black hole.
It lies a staggering 2.44 BILLION light-years away from Earth.
Even at the speed of light (300,000 km/s), a journey there would take 2.44 BILLION years.
The fastest human-made spacecraft, Parker Solar Probe (max 195 km/s), would need 3.9 TRILLION years to reach it.
idea.payoff: We only ever see these cosmic giants as ancient echoes – forever out of reach for physical travel.
idea.title: How Long to Reach a QUASAR? 🤯🌌

scenes.text: Travel to the most LUMINOUS objects?

scenes.text: Meet Quasar 3C 273: fueled by a supermassive black hole.

scenes.text: 2.44 BILLION light-years away. Light speed: 2.44 BILLION years.
scenes.visual: An animated line stretches from a small 'Earth' dot to a distant 'Quasar' point, with numbers appearing.
Stylized illustration of the Parker Solar Probe against a starfield, with an incredibly long, almost infinite, dotted path extending.

scenes.text: Ancient echoes. Forever out of reach.
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, wide shot of deep space, showcasing the vastness and the distant glow of a quasar, emphasizing the travel of light through time.
